title: Protocol & Memory Alignment Audit
description: Cross-reference the current MEMORY.MD against all ADRs to ensure architectural compliance and memory accuracy. Additionally, verify that all scripts in .kntic/hooks/gia/internal/ are consistent with the current project state (no stale references, correct paths, valid ADR checks).

features: Review .kntic/MEMORY.MD for any notes that contradict ADR-001 (State Machine) or ADR-002 (Security).
Clean up the 'Questions for Human' section in .kntic/MEMORY.MD if the answers have been implemented.
Summarize the current 'Project State' to ensure it reflects the latest merged manifests.
Verify that the 'Least Privilege' mounting strategy is correctly documented.
Verify all scripts in .kntic/hooks/gia/internal/ are valid and consistent with current ADRs and project structure.
Confirm internal hook scripts reference correct paths, valid status sets, and current schema expectations.
Report any stale references or drift found in .kntic/hooks/gia/internal/ scripts.
outputs: If discrepancies are found, set status to 'needs_review' and list the conflicts in .kntic/MEMORY.MD.
If internal hook scripts have issues, document them in .kntic/MEMORY.MD § Technical Debt.

actions.summary: Audited all 7 ADRs against MEMORY.MD; fixed ADR table desync (added filenames); documented volume mount strategy; validated all 3 internal hooks — no stale refs or logic issues found.
GIA rejected: Hook(s) failed: 001-adr-compliance.py. Alignment score 0.8333 below threshold 1.0000

Fixed §1 stale merged-task entry; added TD-1 to §3 (orchestrator volume scope); verified all 3 internal hooks — no stale refs, correct paths, valid ADR checks. Previous GIA failure was state.json timing issue (self-resolved).
